Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

I'm a bit undecided whether such a mod-function exit should use "return 0" or "skip".  Previously I asked for "return 0" so that it doesn't report that the test was skipped.  

However, if the start of the test is run without errors but the end is skipped, it is probably more useful to know that part of the test was skipped, since we already know the start of the test *passed* without errors if the skip message is printed instead of an error.

parents.subject: LU-18226 tests: allow small margin for unevicted pages

commit.subject: LU-18321 tests: skip ost-pools/31 for older MDS
commit.message:
LU-18321 tests: skip ost-pools/31 for older MDS

Skip the loop spilling testing in ost-pools test 31 for
MDS < 2.15.50.150 since the test depends on changes made
to the MDS code on the server.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=ost-pools env=ONLY=31 \
  serverversion=2.15.5

Change-Id: I4ad2ded8073eca55363fe60cc49c580ca7d983ae
Fixes: c9c842d678 ("LU-15043 lod: check for spilling loops")
Signed-off-by: Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56657
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Sarah Liu <sarah@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
